compositional_truth_check.details: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/not visible. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only surviving specimen, currently in a state of severe physiological stress.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot is empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos, identified by leaf morphology and rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The specimen shows a single, chlorotic leaf and a thin, etiolated petiole. The plant is suffering from severe light deficiency and likely root-zone compromise, evidenced by the lack of turgor pressure.
plant_audit.P1_P2_P4_Pots: These containers appear to be devoid of viable plant matter, containing only substrate and debris.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears dark and potentially waterlogged or compacted. No signs of active moss or healthy secondary growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of eggshell fragments (calcium amendment attempt) and a small electronic component (IC chip) on the desk surface, indicating non-botanical intervention.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Minimal change; initial state of decline established.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Stagnation; no growth observed, Pothos leaf remains in a state of postural collapse.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The environment appears to have been disturbed; the electronic component has shifted position, and the lighting environment has fluctuated, further stressing the Pothos.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The Pothos is the only remaining biological entity, and its health is poor, characterized by extreme etiolation and loss of structural integrity. The other pots are effectively barren.
anomalies: Presence of an electronic IC chip on the desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil top-dressing.
Total loss of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is in a state of advanced decline. Only one specimen (P3) remains, and it is struggling to survive in a low-light, likely nutrient-depleted environment. The presence of inorganic debris (eggshells, electronics) suggests an experimental or neglected setup. The lack of growth across all time-steps confirms a stagnant, failing ecosystem.
